rationale: The delta touches only marker-scanning internals (scanNonFenceLines/findMarkerLines/hasMarkerLookalike, spliceBriefHeader) and interfaces.md prose -- CreateInput and briefStubBody are byte-identical to round 1 (confirmed by diff: no hunk touches either), so Bead 4's flag-to-field mapping and Bead 5's four stub headings still hold exactly as approved. renderBriefHeader (the 'Your job' block) is also untouched: its reviewer-facing instruction ('verdict: one of APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/REJECT') stays consistent with the amended doc, which adds tally.go's lenient-parsing behavior as informational detail for doc readers without changing what a reviewer is told to write -- no drift. The 'never hand-edited' rewrite correctly threads the pre-Bead-4 reality (skill hand-authors panel.json) and the abandon hand-edit path, which matters for Bead 4/5 since it now explicitly documents that panel.json is skill-authored until Bead 4 lands -- exactly the transition Bead 5 depends on. The two new corrupt-marker error strings are single-line, fixed-text (marker constants + counts only, no user-supplied interpolation), so they're directly wrappable by Bead 4's guard.NewFailure without any control-byte concern. `go test ./internal/panel/...` and `go build ./...` both green, tree clean. My three round-1 notes were correctly carried as non-blocking for Beads 4/5, not re-litigated here.

findings.issue: Verified the two new spliceBriefHeader error messages (whitespace-mangled marker; wrong open/close count) are single-line, contain only fixed marker-constant text plus integers -- safe to wrap in a Bead 4 guard.NewFailure without the control-byte escaping step Bead 4's slug/target validation otherwise needs. No action required.

Round-1 notes (B4 plan's stale `Panel{...}` sample vs the real CreateInput; BRIEF template's Worktree/H1/prior-round fields needing a conscious home when B5 fills stubs; stub headings unpinned by test) are unchanged by this delta and remain for Bead 4/5, per the consolidated round-1 disposition. Not re-raised as new asks.
